help.overview: Find Satellite searches the entire catalog for objects matching a combination of physical, orbital, and observational criteria. It is the fastest way to answer questions like "which rocket bodies from China are in a roughly 100-minute orbit?" or "which payloads are within range of my sensor right now?"
help.imgAlt: The Find Satellite menu with object type, country, orbital, and sensor filters
help.imgCaption: The Find Satellite menu. Every filled-in field narrows the search further.
help.filters:
• Object Type: payloads, rocket bodies, debris. Select multiple types at once; picking a specific type clears "All".
  • Country: one or more launching countries.
  • Satellite Bus / Payload / Shape / Source: physical and data-source characteristics.
  • Azimuth / Elevation / Range: current look angles from the selected sensor. These require a sensor to be selected first.
  • Orbital parameters: Inclination, Period, Right Ascension, and Argument of Perigee.
  • TLE Age: how recently the orbit data was updated, in hours.
  • RCS: radar cross section in m².
Each numeric filter has a Margin field defining the accepted range around your value. Leave it blank to use a sensible default (for example ±5° for angles).
help.howToUse:
1. Open the menu from the bottom bar or press Ctrl+F.
  2. Pick one or more Object Types and Countries from the dropdowns.
  3. Enter numeric criteria and optionally tighten or widen their margins.
  4. Click Find Satellite(s). Matching objects are loaded into the search bar at the top and highlighted on the globe.
  5. Click Export data to save the current results as a CSV file.
help.tip1: Filters combine with AND logic: every criterion must match. If you get no results, remove the most restrictive filter first.
help.tip2: Azimuth, Elevation, and Range are measured from the currently selected sensor, so select a sensor before using them.
help.tip3: Results are capped. If a warning says the limit was hit, narrow your criteria to make sure you are seeing everything.
help.tip4: Because results land in the search bar, you can hand them to any other menu that operates on the current search, or refine them by typing.
